system.name: arq
system.description: Python asyncio job queue library backed by Redis for distributed background task execution.

people.name: Library Consumer
people.description: Python developer who imports arq to enqueue jobs or run a worker process.

people.name: Operator
people.description: Person or CI system that invokes the arq CLI to start and monitor worker processes.

external_systems.name: Redis
external_systems.description: Primary data store; holds job queues (sorted sets), in-progress markers, and job results with TTL.

external_systems.name: PyPI
external_systems.description: Package registry where arq is published; release is triggered via GitHub Actions on tag push.

external_systems.name: Netlify
external_systems.description: Hosts the arq documentation site; docs are published via Makefile publish-docs target on release.

relationships.description: Imports arq to enqueue jobs and configure workers in application code.
Runs arq CLI to start worker processes and check worker health.
Reads and writes job data using Redis sorted sets, hashes, and key-TTL patterns.
Published to PyPI on version tag via GitHub Actions release job.
Documentation site deployed to Netlify via make publish-docs.
